title: Good experience, good benefits, bad priorities
pros: I have had the opportunity to take real ownership for my projects, I've gotten great visibility and resume experience, I have grown as a developer, and the benefits are good. Personally, my direct manager is great.
cons: The company management does not respect tech workers and tends to treat them with contempt. They gloat about how eager they are to fire you and replace you with AI basically every day, even though they have no idea how AI actually works. They only prioritize the quantity of your work, not the quality. I am constantly arguing with unrealistic deadlines from non-technical management who have no patience for any requests to implement things in a more responsible way that will take more time. "AI" is used as a buzzword to replace personal responsibility from people above the VP level.
advice_to_management: You cannot claim to be a tech-oriented company when you treat tech workers as an expense to be squeezed and cut. You also cannot unilaterally simultaneously expand project scope and shorten the deadlines.

title: Too much politics and desi people
pros: Good number of people in the company fo ryou to reach out to and learn from
cons:
- Too much politics. Your code quality, your sde abilities do not matter, the amount of bootlicking you do gets you promoted.
- Dynamic personalities are frowned upon
- What even is mentorship? Your manager will not look out for you, only himself
- Be comfortable with inside jokes, credit stealing, name calling, making fun of others
advice_to_management: Do a deep dive into what your managers are doing over what they are harping about, take into account the words of the engineer too instead of just the managers
